title: Stage 4: Founder Decision
subtitle: The founder compares the direct investor route with the friend-first bridge route before Mission Control takes over.
summary: This stage makes the tradeoff explicit: move directly into the investor-facing route, or choose the softer friend-first bridge path when preserving warmth matters more than speed.
callouts: The direct path is faster and closer to capital allocation.
The friend-first path is softer and protects the relationship when a direct ask feels too early.

likelyInvestorDestination.name: Direct vs friend-first
likelyInvestorDestination.description: Two warm path options are visible before Mission Control receives a payload.

narrative.warmPathSummary: Tigerclaw has already narrowed the graph into two credible routes. The founder now decides whether to take the direct investor path or the softer friend-first bridge path.
narrative.whyThisPerson: This decision stays with the founder because the tradeoff is not only about graph structure. It is also about timing, comfort, and how aggressive the first move should feel.
narrative.whyNow: The system already has enough context to compare both routes clearly, so the remaining question is strategic preference rather than missing information.
narrative.whyThisRankedAboveOthers: Only the direct Harrison route and the friend-first Katia route remain because they are the strongest combinations of warmth, plausibility, and response probability.
narrative.suggestedAsk: Choose the route that best matches the founder's appetite for directness, then hand the selected memo into Mission Control.
supportingProof: The direct route stays closer to investor decision-makers.
The friend-first route preserves warmth and lowers social risk.
Both routes are already grounded in the same saved graph and MiroFish reasoning run.
narrative.likelyObjection: Choosing too quickly can optimize for speed over warmth, or for comfort over decisiveness.
narrative.nextBestStep: Select one route and continue to Mission Control with that memo.
narrative.draftMessage: Choose the route first. Mission Control should only receive the final memo after the founder commits to direct or friend-first.

executionRows.label: Stage mode
executionRows.value: Compare and choose between the two remaining warm paths.

executionRows.label: Execution posture
executionRows.value: Mission Control remains downstream of the founder decision.
